trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at score 50 (3.45ms reference, 3.69ms current Triton impl). Profiling shows conv3d 90.5%, max_pool3d 6.5%, logsumexp 5.6%. The Triton kernel has excessive launch overhead from 1M+ programs. Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: You've spent time exploring the file structure but haven't run the benchmark yet. Please run the baseline benchmark now: `/opt/venv/bin/python3 /workspace/test_harness.py --level 2 --problem-id 43` to establish a baseline score. Also read `test_harness.py` to understand how it expects the solution file to be structured.

Since conv3d dominates runtime and your custom Triton kernel only covers the small logsumexp+relu portion, try using `torch.compile(mode="default")` on the entire pure PyTorch forward (conv3d → maxpool → logsumexp → relu) **without** any custom Triton kernel. This lets the compiler optimize/fuse the entire pipeline including the conv3d. Your `ModelNew.forward` can just be the same as `Model.forward` but wrapped with `torch.compile`.

Don't give up — score 50 means correct but no speedup yet. Two key optimizations: (1) Reduce launch overhead by having each Triton program handle a tile of spatial locations (e.g., BLOCK_SIZE=256 elements per program) instead of 1 per program, shrinking your grid from 1M to ~4K programs. (2) Fuse maxpool3d + logsumexp + relu into a single Triton kernel to eliminate the intermediate tensor write/read between maxpool and logsumexp — this saves significant memory bandwidth. The maxpool over a 2×2×2 window with 16 output channels feeding into logsumexp is a reasonable amount of work per output element for a fused kernel.
